reviews.review_title: It's good, but It needs improvement...
reviews.review_description:
Here are some improvements you can make:
1. Fix the lighting so it's not so bright
2. Fix the sound effects and monster sounds so they are not so blasting loud, like when the monster runs to u in an explosion in floors 1 and 
3. Add more maps, levels, modes, and floors
4. Fix player movement so you don't get stuck on everything (but keep the player being fast)
5. Add items to fight the monster
6. Finally, fix bugs and other things in general

I hope this feedback helps you to make this game better now that you're putting your full effort into it

As someone who’s spent a good amount of time in Ape Redux, I can honestly say it’s one of the most fun VR games I’ve played on Meta Quest. The movement feels smooth and satisfying, and once you get the hang of it, swinging and climbing around the map feels natural. Every match turns into pure chaos in the best way possible — whether I’m chasing someone down or barely escaping, it always ends up being hilarious. It’s one of those games where you lose track of time because you’re just having fun the whole session.

What really keeps me coming back is the multiplayer. Every lobby feels different depending on who you’re playing with, and the social aspect makes everything way more entertaining. The maps are big enough to explore but not overwhelming, and there’s always something going on. You can tell that MonoSpark Studios focused on making the gameplay feel fun first, and it shows.

Overall, I’d easily give Ape Redux 5 stars. It’s simple to get into, fun to master, and it delivers nonstop laughs with friends or even random players. For a VR game, it really nails what makes the platform exciting — movement, freedom, and unpredictable moments.

reviews.review_title: Wow, What Did I Just Play? /n
reviews.review_description:
As politely as I could put it, this game just was not the best. I played it alongside fellow reviewer Loganington, and we both ended up with negative opinions on it.
The map was somewhat decent, being fairly large and (slightly) interesting. The items are just sort of strange, and the amount of monster trackers that spawn are absurd. The little things walking around the map are confusing and unnecessary, and pull away from the fear factor of the game and deteriorate the scary atmosphere.
The lobbies—there are no private lobbies or even a computer to change your color or name, as well as no option to turn on snap turning.
The monsters are just bad. From animations to model, you do not need to leave fire where you walk. They are INSANELY loud with no need to be that loud, and aren’t even scary.
I have some positive things to say; the game didn’t feel laggy, and the one public server me and Loganington were forced into worked.
Besides that, the game really isn’t worth your time and energy, and is just like every other basic bad horror game with Gorilla Tag movement.
